tools.name: search_memories
tools.description: Searches semantically across stored memories using Mem0. Use this to retrieve relevant context about user preferences, patterns, and past interactions before responding. This is for retrieval only - use add_memory to store new memories, and list_memories to see all memories.

query.description: Search query text to find relevant memories

limit.description: Maximum number of memories to return

user.description: User ID to search memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: add_memory
tools.description: Adds new memories to Mem0 storage. Use this when user explicitly asks to remember something or to store conversation context for future retrieval. Supports two forms: simple text memory or conversation messages (auto-extracts memories via LLM). For searching stored memories, use search_memories instead.

text.description: Simple text memory to store (use instead of messages)

messages.description: Array of conversation messages to extract memories from (use instead of text)

user.description: User ID to store memory for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: list_memories
tools.description: Lists all stored memories for a user with IDs and creation dates. Use this to see what memories are currently stored. For searching specific memories, use search_memories instead.

user.description: User ID to list memories for (defaults to configured user)

tools.name: delete_memory
tools.description: Deletes memories from Mem0 storage. Use this to remove specific memories or all memories for a user. Supports two modes: delete a specific memory by ID, or delete all memories for a user.

memory_id.description: ID of specific memory to delete (use instead of all flag)

all.description: Delete all memories for the user (use instead of memory_id)

user.description: User ID to delete memories for (required when using all flag)

messages.content: Hey, I wanted to jot down some notes about Marcus Thompson, one of our college players I've been working with. He's a wide receiver with some real strengths but also areas where he needs to develop. Can you help me record his key skills and where he needs to improve?
I'd be happy to help you record notes about Marcus Thompson! Just go ahead and share the details about his strengths and areas for development, and I'll store them for you.

function_call.name: skill-tools-add_memory
function_call.arguments: {"text": "Notes on Marcus Thompson (college WR): Strengths: [User to provide], Areas for improvement: [User to provide]"}

messages.content: {"status": "success", "tool": "add_memory", "memory_id": "mem_mt_001", "stored_text": "Notes on Marcus Thompson (college WR): Strengths: [User to provide], Areas for improvement: [User to provide]"}
messages.name: skill-tools-add_memory

messages.content: I see I jumped the gun a bit! I stored a placeholder, but I didn't actually capture the specific details you wanted to record. Could you share Marcus Thompson's key strengths and the areas where he needs to develop? Once you provide those details, I'll store the actual notes for you.

Sure, let me give you those details now. Marcus has excellent route-running ability and great hands - he catches almost everything thrown his way. He's also got solid speed and can create separation against most college cornerbacks. Where he needs work is his blocking, especially in run support, and he tends to disappear in games when the offense is struggling. He's also got to get stronger if he wants to compete at the next level.

function_call.arguments: {"text": "Notes on Marcus Thompson (college WR): Strengths: Excellent route-running ability, great hands (catches almost everything), solid speed, can create separation against most college cornerbacks. Areas for improvement: Blocking (especially in run support), tends to disappear in games when offense is struggling, needs to get stronger to compete at the next level."}

Areas for improvement: Blocking (especially in run support), tends to disappear in games when offense is struggling, needs to get stronger to compete at the next level.", "user": "default"}

messages.content:
Done! I've recorded Marcus Thompson's player notes. Here's what I stored:

**Strengths:**
- Excellent route-running ability
- Great hands (catches almost everything)
- Solid speed
- Creates separation against most college cornerbacks

**Areas for Improvement:**
- Blocking (especially in run support)
- Tends to disappear when the offense is struggling
- Needs to get stronger to compete at the next level

You can reference these notes anytime — just ask and I'll pull them up!
Can you show me all the notes you've stored so far? I want to make sure everything was recorded correctly.

function_call.name: skill-tools-search_memories
function_call.arguments: {"query": "Marcus Thompson wide receiver college player notes"}

messages.content:
That's odd — I'm not seeing any stored memories, even the ones we just added. This might be a technical issue with the storage system. 

The good news is I still have the information you provided fresh in this conversation. Would you like me to re-add the notes, or would you prefer to try again later? I can also show you exactly what was recorded so you can confirm it matches what you want stored.

Yeah, let's re-add those notes since it looks like they didn't save properly. Once they're in there, I want to go through and make sure we don't have any old or outdated information hanging around - maybe some notes from previous seasons or anything that's no longer accurate for Marcus. Can you add the notes again and then help me clean up anything that needs to be removed?
